title: Nvidia releases open AI models for quantum computing tasks 'Ising' said to be 2.5x faster and 3x more accurate than existing tools for decoding
summary: Nvidia has released Ising, an open-source AI model for quantum computing. It claims to be 2.5x faster and 3x more accurate than existing tools for decoding. Ising is designed for quantum processor calibration and real-time error correction.
insight: Speeding up quantum computing with AI can improve accuracy significantly.

title: Anthropic's Claude Mythos might be the best overall AI model for cybersecurity, but cheaper models can attain similar results, research shows cross-examination of the frontier model raises questions on uptime and reliability
summary: Anthropic's Mythos may be the best cybersecurity AI, but it's pricey and not the only option.
insight: Cost-effectiveness trumps cutting-edge AI for many cybersecurity applications.

title: Sony launches 720 Hz dual-mode OLED QHD gaming monitor 27-inch Inzone M10S II is aimed at hardcore eSports gamers
summary: Sony's 27-inch Inzone M10S II gaming monitor boasts a 720 Hz refresh rate at 720p resolution, targeting hardcore eSports gamers.
insight: Ultra-high refresh rate enhances competitive gaming experience dramatically.

title: Intel's promising upcoming Nova Lake-S lineup reportedly includes an iGPU-focused SKU with 12 Xe3P cores new leak claims a midrange 16-core CPU with powerful integrated graphics is in the works
summary: A new leak suggests Intel's Nova Lake-S lineup will include a 16-core CPU with 12 Xe3P iGPU cores, targeting the Ryzen G-series APUs. This would offer significantly more integrated graphics capabilities than current AMD offerings. The 16-core CPU would be a strong competitor in the mid-range market.
insight: Intel's iGPU-focused SKU could surpass AMD's offerings in graphics performance.

title: Nvidia quashes rumor it s planning to purchase a major PC manufacturer says that it s not engaged in discussions to acquire any PC maker
summary: Nvidia denied acquiring another PC manufacturer despite 2024 rumors. The company stated it's not in talks to acquire any PC maker. Nvidia's clarification aims to alleviate market uncertainty.
insight: Nvidia's acquisition plans affect its position in the PC hardware market.

title: China's premiere memory-maker YMTC plans two additional Wuhan fabs using homegrown chipmaking tools Phase 3 crosses 50% domestic tooling threshold
summary: YMTC plans to build two additional fabs in Wuhan using homegrown chipmaking tools.
insight: China's self-reliance in chip production gains significant momentum domestically.

title: Bambu Lab X2D review: Improving a fan favorite
summary: The Bambu Lab X2D is an upgraded version of the X1 3D printer, featuring improved performance and reliability. It addresses user feedback and incorporates new technologies to enhance the printing experience. The X2D aims to solidify its position as a top-choice 3D printer.
insight: Refined design and performance improve user experience significantly

title: Alienware AW2726DM 27-inch QHD 240 Hz QD-OLED gaming monitor review: A price breakthrough for desktop OLED
summary: Alienware's AW2726DM breaks price barriers with its 27-inch QHD QD-OLED monitor, offering high-quality features at an affordable price of $350. This monitor boasts 240 Hz refresh rate, Adaptive-Sync, HDR10, and wide gamut color. It's a significant breakthrough for desktop OLED technology.
insight: Affordable OLED gaming monitors for the masses

title: Our experts review your astonishing PC builds and setups in Rig Rundown from wall-mounted setups to a system packed inside of a 1:6 scale RC car
summary: Tom's Hardware showcases user-submitted PC builds and crowns a winner in the inaugural Tom's Hardware Premium Rig Rundown results. The publication goes through various unique and creative setups, from wall-mounted PCs to a system packed inside of a 1:6 scale RC car. This special issue highlights the art of PC customization and innovation.
insight: Creative PC builds showcase innovation and customization expertise.

title: News outlets are blocking Wayback Machine from archiving their pages 23 outlets concerned AI companies might abuse fair use and use it to train their models
summary: Major news outlets block Wayback Machine due to fair use concerns, fearing AI companies will misuse publicly available data to train their models.
insight: Fair use laws may be exploited for AI model training purposes

title: Mark Zuckerberg reportedly working on AI clone of himself Meta insiders claim 3D photoreal animated Zuck will be able to engage with employees on his behalf
summary: Mark Zuckerberg is reportedly working on AI clone to engage employees, Meta insiders claim. The 3D photorealistic animated replica will be able to communicate on his behalf. This development raises questions about delegation of authority.
insight: Redefining corporate leadership through AI-powered delegation and impersonation capabilities

title: Score a massive $700 off this 4K-ready Lenovo gaming PC with an RTX 5070 Ti, now just $1,899 epic Legion Tower 5i pre-built ships with a 20-core Intel CPU, 32GB DDR5 and a 2TB SSD
summary: Get a high-performance gaming PC with 20-core Intel CPU, 32GB DDR5 RAM, and 2TB SSD, now at a discounted price of $1,899.99.
insight: High-end gaming capabilities at a relatively affordable price point.

title: Veteran Windows dev shows off AI running on 47-year-old PDP11 with 6 MHz CPU and 64KB of RAM 'gloriously absurd' project runs transformer model written in PDP-11 assembly language
summary: A veteran Windows developer has successfully trained a neural network on a 47-year-old PDP-11 minicomputer with a 6 MHz CPU and 64 KB of RAM, showcasing the 'gloriously absurd' capabilities of AI on outdated hardware.
insight: Even ancient hardware can run AI with careful optimization and assembly language.

title: Half of all US employees now use artificial intelligence at work, crossing landmark threshold for first time Gallup data shows daily and weekly usage hitting all-time high of 28% in Q1 2026, with 65% feeling positive about its impact on productivity
summary: Half of US employees use AI at work, with 28% daily/weekly usage. A Gallup survey shows a positive impact on productivity. Employees' confidence in AI's benefits is on the rise.
insight: Widespread AI adoption boosts employee productivity and confidence.

title: China has spent 3.6 times more than the US on chipmaking subsidies over the past decade $142 billion and counting, easily outweighs CHIPS Act
summary: China's semiconductor subsidies exceeded US spending by 3.6 times from 2014-2023, totaling $142 billion. This significant investment in chipmaking has given China a competitive edge. The US CHIPS Act, which committed $39 billion, is dwarfed by China's spending.
insight: China's massive chip subsidies create a significant competitive imbalance.

title: Clicking "reject cookies" might not actually do anything
summary: Tech giants continue to track users despite 'reject cookies' settings.
insight: Browser settings may not be enough to prevent tracking.

title: DaVinci Resolve 21 beta adds photo editing and deeper AI integration
summary: DaVinci Resolve 21 beta adds photo editing with AI-powered enhancements, integrating seamlessly into existing workflows.
insight: Enhanced color grading and AI-driven photo editing increases creative possibilities.

title: Malware campaign lures users with fake Windows Update website
summary: Malwarebytes uncovered a malicious campaign targeting French-speaking Windows Update users, using layered obfuscation to deliver malicious payloads and steal sensitive data.
insight: French-speaking users at risk of targeted malware attacks through fake Windows Update websites.

title: Apple is testing four smart glasses designs as it prepares to challenge Meta Ray-Bans
summary: Apple is developing its own smart glasses internally, unlike Meta's collaboration with Ray-Ban.
insight: Apple's independent approach sets it apart in the smart glasses market.

title: Amazon purchases Globalstar for $11.6B to expand its low Earth orbit satellite network
summary: Amazon acquires Globalstar for $11.6B to expand its low Earth orbit satellite network, gaining control over existing operations, supporting infrastructure, and related assets.
insight: Amazon expands satellite capabilities for potential Project Kuiper success

title: Capcom's Pragmata earns strong early reviews ahead of release
summary: Pragmata earns strong reviews for its innovative third-person shooting mechanics and real-time hacking features, combined with stunning futuristic visuals and engaging combat depth.
insight: Capcom's Pragmata offers a fresh gaming experience with hacking mechanics.

title: Microsoft is removing 32GB size limit for FAT32 volumes, this time for real
summary: Microsoft has released a Windows 11 preview build with a new, higher FAT32 size limit, improving compatibility and flexibility.
insight: FAT32 size limit increase enhances compatibility and flexibility for users.

title: Missouri town ousts half of its city council after $6 billion AI data center approval
summary: Festus, Missouri, ousts half of its city council after approving a $6 billion AI data center project. The 360-acre development faced local objections despite being touted as a 'once-in-a-generation windfall'.
insight: Community pushback can lead to significant changes in local government leadership.

title: External GPUs were always second best. CopprLink may change that
summary: CopprLink offers a high-bandwidth interface with 64 GB/s throughput, surpassing current eGPU options.
insight: Faster data transfer may revolutionize eGPU performance and adoption

title: Google will demote websites that hijack your browser's back button
summary: Google will demote websites that hijack the back button, considering it a malicious practice.
insight: Google prioritizes user control and intuitive browsing experiences.

title: Japan finds a way to recover 90% of lithium from old EV batteries
summary: Japan's JX Metals Circular Solutions has developed a process to recover 90% of lithium from old EV batteries, a breakthrough announced in April 2025. The company's plant in Tsuruga is at the center of the innovative technology. This achievement could significantly improve EV recycling and battery sustainability.
insight: Revolutionizing EV recycling with high lithium recovery rates possible

title: Man who vandalized Sam Altman's home claimed AI would end humanity, charged with attempted murder
summary: A 20-year-old man vandalized Sam Altman's home and threatened OpenAI's office, claiming AI would end humanity. He threw a flaming bottle at the gate and was detained outside the OpenAI office. The incident has sparked concerns about AI-related threats.
insight: Vandalism raises concerns about AI-related violence and potential threats.

title: New terahertz technique lets engineers see inside running processors in real time
summary: Researchers modified a vector network analyzer to operate in the terahertz domain, enabling real-time monitoring of processors.
insight: Insights into processor behavior in real-time could greatly improve chip design and reliability.

title: Microsoft just made its Surface laptops a lot more expensive
summary: Microsoft has increased prices for its Surface Pro and Laptop models by $300, citing rising component costs.
insight: Higher prices may deter buyers and impact Surface sales

title: Shipping records suggest Valve will launch the Steam Controller before the Steam Machine
summary: Valve's shipping records suggest the Steam Controller may release before Steam Machine.
insight: Implications for Steam's gaming ecosystem and user experience.

title: This 3D-printed 15-fan side panel drops CPU temps by 20 degrees
summary: A 3D-printed side panel with 15 fans lowers CPU temperatures by 20 degrees. This design replaces tempered glass with a fan-filled structure for improved airflow. It was created in response to user feedback.
insight: Optimized airflow significantly reduces CPU temperatures with effective fan integration.
